title: Paste handler missing redraw_panel — fixed-panel grows but DECSTBM is never reissued
task:
Regression introduced in commit bf65471f (feat(norn-tui): handle bracketed multi-line paste as a single editor draft). The new Event::Paste handlers call `sync_input_for_current_geometry` and `render_input`, but DO NOT call `redraw_panel`. This means: when paste grows the editor from 1 line to N lines, the fixed panel marks itself height-dirty via set_input_area, but the terminal's DECSTBM scroll region is never reissued. The terminal still believes the scroll region extends to the original (smaller) bottom margin, so the new visual rows painted by render_input overflow into rows the terminal treats as scroll region. Result: the pasted content appears once in the overflow space and a second time in the panel paint below, with the panel separator visible between them. When the user then deletes characters and the panel shrinks, the stale paint from the previous, larger panel position is not cleared.

OUTCOME — the user pastes a 20-line block:
- The fixed panel grows to fit the pasted text (capped at INPUT_AREA_MAX_ROWS = 12 visual rows; long pastes scroll internally — this is already correctly wired by the visual-wrap fix).
- DECSTBM is reissued so the terminal's scroll region bottom margin matches the new panel top.
- The pasted text appears EXACTLY ONCE in the input panel — no duplicate copy in the area above the panel separator.
- Deleting characters and shrinking the editor back down repaints cleanly: the panel shrinks, the rows that transition from panel back to scroll region are cleared, no stale paint is left behind.

requirements.description: Add `redraw_panel(state, guard)?;` between `sync_input_for_current_geometry(state, guard);` and `render_input(state, guard)?;` in the outer Event::Paste arm of dispatch_event in crates/norn-tui/src/app/event_loop.rs (currently lines 375-380). This mirrors the existing pattern used by the Event::WindowResized arm three lines below it.
requirements.acceptance: The outer Event::Paste arm has the same three-call sequence as Event::WindowResized: sync_input_for_current_geometry, redraw_panel, render_input. cargo test -p norn-tui passes; existing paste tests in event_loop.rs (paste_inserts_multiline_text_and_dismisses_popup_without_turn, paste_splices_at_cursor_and_parks_after_inserted_text) still pass — they don't exercise the rendering path, so the call addition does not regress them.

requirements.description: Apply the same fix to the mid-turn Event::Paste arm in crates/norn-tui/src/app/event_loop.rs (currently around line 560, inside the `else if let Event::Paste(text) = event` block). The mid-turn handler must also reissue DECSTBM when paste grows the panel mid-stream.
requirements.acceptance: The mid-turn paste handler has the same redraw_panel call. cargo clippy -p norn-tui --all-targets -- -D warnings clean. cargo test -p norn-tui passes.

requirements.description: Audit the symmetric shrink path. When the editor shrinks (Backspace at the start of a line removing a newline, delete_to_line_start, delete_word_back across a line boundary, the apply_acceptance after an autocomplete accept that replaces multiple lines with a shorter span, the existing delete actions inside apply_edit_action), the post-edit sequence already routes through `handle_action` which calls `redraw_all` — that path correctly reissues DECSTBM via redraw_panel. CONFIRM this by reading handle_action's tail in event_loop.rs and the redraw_all/redraw_panel implementation in app/render.rs. If any edit path that shrinks the editor does NOT route through redraw_all/redraw_panel, fix it. Document the audit result in the structured-output report (concerns array) listing every set_input_area site and whether it pairs with a redraw_panel before the next render_input.
requirements.acceptance: A short audit list in the concerns array or in docs/reports/tui-set-input-area-callsites.md identifying every set_input_area call site and confirming it pairs with a redraw_panel before render_input. No code change required if all paths already pair correctly — the audit is the deliverable.

requirements.description: Add a regression test that paste THEN delete leaves the panel in the correct shrunken state. The test does not need to inspect the painted bytes (that's hard without a real terminal); instead assert the bookkeeping: starting from an empty editor, simulate a paste of 'line1\nline2\nline3', then simulate enough backspaces to remove all of line3 and the joining newline so the editor is back to 2 lines, then verify state.fixed_panel.total_height() is the value visual_height(2_logical_lines, cols) would produce. The point of the test is to catch a future regression where set_input_area gets called with a logical-line count instead of visual-row count or where the shrink path stops being wired.
requirements.acceptance: A new unit test passes. It uses insert_paste_text + a sequence of editor.backspace() calls, then asserts the fixed_panel input area reflects the SHRUNKEN visual height. cargo test -p norn-tui passes.

context:
FILES:
- crates/norn-tui/src/app/event_loop.rs — Event::Paste arms in dispatch_event (outer, ~line 375) and the mid-turn loop (~line 560). The Event::WindowResized arm at ~line 381 is the reference pattern: sync → redraw_panel → render_input.
- crates/norn-tui/src/app/render.rs — redraw_all, redraw_panel, render_input. redraw_panel internally reissues DECSTBM via guard.reissue_scroll_region when fixed_panel.height_dirty() is true.
- crates/norn-tui/src/render/fixed_panel.rs — FixedPanel.set_input_area marks height_dirty; height_dirty is consumed by redraw_panel.

WHY THIS REGRESSED — the bracketed-paste task (committed bf65471f) added the Event::Paste handlers from scratch, modelling them on render_input + sync_input_for_current_geometry pattern. The author overlooked that render_input alone never reissues DECSTBM — it just paints visual rows at the precomputed input_top. Only redraw_panel does the DECSTBM reissue. The bracketed-paste task didn't include rendering-path tests; the visual artifacts only show up against a real terminal, which the unit tests cannot reproduce.

WHAT'S ALREADY CORRECT — DO NOT REIMPLEMENT:
- The visual-wrap model (capped_input_height, sync_input_area, scroll_to_cursor, viewport_top)
- The bracketed paste enable/reset and the insert_paste_text helper
- The redraw_panel internals (DECSTBM reissue + panel-shrink row clear)
- The Event::WindowResized handler pattern

DO NOT ADD A SEPARATE TEST FOR THE GROW PAINT — the rendering correctness is hard to assert without a real terminal. The bookkeeping test in R4 (panel size after paste+delete) catches the regression cheaply.

STANDARDS — per /Users/tom/Developer/ablative/yggdrasil/CLAUDE.md: no unwrap/expect/panic/todo in production; pedantic clippy clean; no TODO/FIXME/HACK; thiserror in library code.

VERIFICATION: cargo check -p norn-tui; cargo clippy -p norn-tui --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p norn-tui; grep TODO/FIXME/HACK in changed files.
